title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Buildkit Llb Graph
summary: A CI build is slow, leaks credentials through configuration, and ships compilers that production never needs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker buildkit llb graph increment in cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Buildkit Llb Graph · Theory Docker Buildkit Llb Graph
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Buildkit Llb Graph contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters. A defensible solution applies Docker Buildkit Llb Graph, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-buildkit-llb-graph — Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-4
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: worked docker buildkit cache multistage 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: BuildKit executes every Dockerfile line sequentially and always emits a local image. This fails the Docker Buildkit Llb Graph contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
